item.title: Brazilian Real Becomes ‘Collateral Damage’ as Dollar Roars Back
item.description: The Brazilian real is heading for its worst month this year as a rebound in the dollar and shifting interest-rate expectations push investors to unwind one of their most favored carry trades.

item.title: AI Won’t Be Substitute for Human Workers, OpenAI Economist Says
item.description: The rise of artificial intelligence won’t make human workers superfluous, according to OpenAI Chief Economist Ronnie Chatterji.

item.title: India Says Growth Outlook Brightens as Middle East Risks Ease
item.description: India’s growth prospects have brightened following the recent de-esclation in the Middle East conflict, as lower oil prices reduced risks to inflation and the external balance, said the government’s monthly report.

item.title: SEC Mulls New ETF Rules as $16 Trillion Boom Disrupts Status Quo
item.description: The US Securities and Exchange Commission is signaling a potential rethink of how it oversees exchange-traded funds after a recent wave of filings for prediction-market ETFs prompted fresh scrutiny of the existing regulatory framework.

item.title: Venezuela Bond Rally Fades as Investors Price in Bleaker Outlook
item.description: Venezuelan bonds are tumbling as investors factor in a bleaker financial outlook after two devastating earthquakes added to the challenges of a sweeping debt restructuring.

item.title: US Consumer Confidence Inches Up, Helped by Lower Gas Prices
item.description: US consumer confidence edged up in June, as falling gas prices offset concerns about the labor market.

item.title: US Job Openings Tick Up in Sign of Steady Labor Demand
item.description: US job openings were little changed in May, suggesting labor demand remains stable alongside a recent pickup in payroll growth.

item.title: AI Chip Startup Etched Says Jane Street, TSMC-Linked VC Invested
item.description: Nvidia rival says it has raised $800 million and has $1 billion in contracts.

item.title: US Stocks Advance as Indexes Head for Best Quarter Since 2020
item.description: US stocks advanced on Tuesday as the main indexes zeroed in on their best quarter in six years and traders parsed the latest economic data.

item.title: UK ‘Minded to Intervene’ in Paramount’s Warner Bros. Deal Over Media Ownership Concerns
item.description: Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. could face an intervention from the UK government after the country’s culture and media secretary raised concerns over the diversity of media ownership.

item.title: Citadel Securities Sees Retail Crowd Buying Dips at Record Pace
item.description: Mom-and-pop investors have been bargain hunting at a record pace so far this year, according to Scott Rubner of Citadel Securities.

item.title: Russia Boosts Crude Oil Exports to Record While Prices Tumble
item.description: Despite surging shipments, Moscow's oil earnings have dropped to the lowest since March

item.title: South Africa Court Drops Credit Suisse From Rand-Fixing Suit
item.description: South Africa’s Constitutional Court dismissed Credit Suisse Group AG from a suit alleging that the Swiss bank and others had manipulated the rand-dollar exchange rate for six years through at least September 2013.

item.title: CME Launches New Beef Contracts as US Cattle Prices Surge
item.description: CME Group is launching trading contracts for the beef products used in hamburgers and meatballs, as prices surge amid a shrinking US cattle herd.

item.title: The Thing to Watch in Thursday’s Jobs Report
item.description: Will wage growth start to pick up?

item.title: Canada Economy Set for Second Quarter Rebound, Breaking Slump
item.description: Canada’s economy is set to rebound sharply in the second quarter amid a spike in oil production, breaking a half-year of stagnation.

item.title: AI Leverage Is More Worrying Than Valuations, IMF’s Adrian Says
item.description: Artificial intelligence debt issuance may point to more of a financial stability concern than stock valuations, according to a senior International Monetary Fund official.

item.title: Copper Traders Gear Up for Another Round of Tariff Turmoil
item.description: The US Commerce Department is due to deliver a key report to the White House to inform Donald Trump’s next move.

item.title: German Inflation Eases as Oil Retreat Tames Prices in Europe
item.description: German inflation cooled more than expected in June, part of a slowdown across most of the region brought on by the drop in global oil prices.

item.title: Argentina’s Beef Export Boom to US Leaves Locals Paying More for Steak
item.description: Ranchers on the Pampas answer Trump’s call and turn away from China, but the trend doesn’t bode well for Milei as he heads into an election.

item.title: Europe’s Top Steelmakers Warn Against Weakening Carbon Market
item.description: The European Union’s biggest steelmakers, including Salzgitter AG and SSAB AB, urged policy makers to avoid weakening the bloc’s flagship emissions market and to support strengthening its carbon border levy.

item.title: Trump Tariff Defeat Is Market Tailwind ‘No One Is Talking About’
item.description: The sweeping tariffs underpinning President Donald Trump’s economic policy may have gone from a headwind to a tailwind for the stock market, with the Supreme Court’s decision to unwind them setting the stage for a potential earnings boon for some companies.

item.title: Armani Taps BCG as Fashion Group Adapts to Post-Founder Era
item.description: Giorgio Armani SpA is working with Boston Consulting Group on ways to grow in a few key luxury segments before a stake-sale process is set to kick off later this year, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 30, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures climb 0.1% as of 7:40 a.m. in New York on the last day of a quarter that looks set to be the S&P 500’s best in six years.

item.title: BOE’s Breeden Warns AI Agents Risk Causing Market Meltdowns
item.description: Autonomous artificial intelligence agents risk causing “market meltdown” and may need tighter regulation, Bank of England Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden warned.

item.title: Emerging Market Bond Sales Hit $450 Billion as Spreads Narrow
item.description: Total sales of international bonds by emerging-market issuers hit a record in the first half of 2026 as sovereigns and corporations take advantage of the tightest spreads in almost two decades.

item.title: Call Center Stocks Fall on Worry AI Makes Them ‘Uninvestible’
item.description: Signs that companies are increasingly turning to artificial intelligence to handle customer service tasks has triggered a fresh selloff in shares of two call-center companies.

item.title: Jon and Mindy Gray Bet on AI Science Research to Prevent Cancer
item.description: Blackstone Inc. President Jon Gray has steered his firm to more artificial-intelligence investments, and the same is true with his science philanthropy.

item.title: Chinese AI Firms’ Newest Battleground: World Cup Predictions
item.description: The companies are using interest in the popular sporting event to promote their AI models

item.title: Rate Hike Gives ECB Sense of Confidence
item.description: In 2022, the Sintra forum had embarrassment in the air. Now policymakers are more relaxed.
